summary: This is a brief introductory note about affordances and metaphors in UI design. Spolsky frames both concepts as tools for aligning the user's mental model with how the program actually works. The post serves primarily as a teaser directing readers to a longer treatment in Chapter 4 of his 'UI for Programmers' series.
key_points: Affordances and metaphors are the two central concepts in UI design discourse
Both serve the same fundamental purpose: aligning the user's mental model with the program's actual model
They work by providing 'little clues' about how the program works and what users should do
money_quotes: Two related concepts you hear about endlessly in UI design are affordances and metaphors.
These are just ways to bring the user model in line with the program model by giving the user little clues about how the program works and what they're supposed to do with it.
key_insight: Affordances and metaphors in UI design both serve the same goal: closing the gap between how users think a program works and how it actually works.
